mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows consistent growth but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising federal funds rates and a sharp decline in housing starts indicates a restrictive monetary policy impacting interest-sensitive sectors, which typically precedes broader economic slowdowns and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25% MoM AND HOUST falls by > 10% MoM
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.25% MoM and HOUST falls by > 10% MoM, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, accelerating industrial production indicates a rebound in manufacturing and overall economic activity, which supports corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows accelerating growth during a recovery but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in consumer prices signals accelerating inflation, which can erode corporate profit margins and prompt tighter monetary policy, leading to short-term negative S&P 500 reactions over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.5% MoM, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent growth but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong year-over-year growth in real GDP and industrial production indicates a broad-based and healthy economic expansion, signaling strong corporate fundamentals and sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2% YoY AND INDPRO growth > 1% YoY
failure_condition: If both GDPC1 and INDPRO show strong YoY growth, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained reduction in the federal funds rate by the central bank signals an easing monetary policy, which typically lowers borrowing costs for businesses and stimulates economic activity, leading to positive short-term equity market reactions over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS falls by more than 0.25% over 3 months, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.
